text: Lancet Countdown: Tracking Progress on Health and Climate Change Tracking the connections between climate change and health in Europe The report represents the work of 65 experts from 46 institutions, monitoring progress across 43 indicators. Discover the latest findings at a national level Highlighting the most relevant themes and data by country.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: The Lancet Countdown is an international research collaboration, funded by Wellcome and headquartered at University College London (UCL). Bringing together over 300 researchers from leading research institutions and UN agencies across the globe, we work to ensure that health is at the centre of how decision makers understand and respond to climate change.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: Through high-quality scientific data, we work to inform policies that can maximise the health benefits of climate change action, and enable a world in which all populations can thrive. The Lancet Countdown operates with strategic and financial support from Wellcome, a politically and financially independent global charitable foundation.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: Wellcome supports science to solve the urgent health issues facing everyone. Its Climate and Health programme seeks to generate the health evidence for policy action that protects health from climate change. The 2015 Lancet Commission concluded that ‘tackling climate change could be the greatest global health opportunity of the 21st century’.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: In 2016 the Lancet Countdown was formed to provide a global academic collaboration with the ability to track and monitor progress on health and climate change, and engage policymakers and health professionals in maximising this opportunity. Our work is led by almost 300 health professionals, scientists and researchers, at more than 50 leading academic centres and UN agencies across the world.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: The collaboration brings together world-class experts from a range of disciplines, including health and epidemiology, climate science, engineering, agriculture, transport, economics, and social and political sciences. The Lancet Countdown is made up of individuals and organisations from all over the world, that are committed to changing the way we think about climate change and health.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change

text: Our Regional Centres in Asia, Africa, Europe, Oceania, Small Island Developing States (SIDS) and Latin America expand on our global data monitoring and analysis, looking at the local and national implications on public health.
source: Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change
